seoTitle: How to Use the Google Sheets Typeform Integration
metaDescription: Learn how to automatically sync Typeform responses to Google Sheets so your data stays organized without manual entry.
intro: Manually transferring form responses into a spreadsheet takes time and introduces the risk of errors. This guide covers how to connect Typeform with Google Sheets so new submissions are automatically added, keeping your data organized and ready for analysis.

steps.heading: Open Typeform Integrations
steps.body: Go to your Typeform account's integrations section to find the Google Sheets connection option.

steps.heading: Connect Google Sheets
steps.body: Authenticate your Google account and grant Typeform access to your Google Sheets.

steps.heading: Select spreadsheet and configure sync
steps.body: Choose the spreadsheet where responses should be sent, map the form fields to columns, and enable automatic syncing for new submissions.

faq.question: Does this sync every new response automatically?
faq.answer: Yes, once configured, new Typeform submissions are automatically added to the connected Google Sheets spreadsheet.

faq.question: Can I use this integration for lead generation reporting?
faq.answer: Yes, it's commonly used for lead generation, customer feedback, survey responses, registrations, and general reporting.

faq.question: What happens if I add a new question to my form?
faq.answer: You may need to update the field mapping so the new question's responses are correctly directed to a column in your spreadsheet.

faq.question: Can my team collaborate on the synced data?
faq.answer: Yes, since the data lands in Google Sheets, your team can collaborate on analysis using standard spreadsheet sharing and permissions.
